revision.comment: 出羽における平安時代の俘囚の反乱
revision.text:
'''天慶の乱'''（てんぎょうのらん）は[[平安時代]]に起きた[[俘囚]]（[[蝦夷]]）の反乱である。
== 経緯 == [[天慶]]2年[[4月17日 (旧暦)|4月17日]]（[[939年]][[5月8日]]）に俘囚が反乱し、秋田城の軍と交戦したことが[[平安京|都]]に伝わったと『[[日本紀略]]』等に見える。『[[貞信公記]]抄』によると、反乱した俘囚は[[秋田郡]]に到り官稲を奪ったとある。同書ではこのとき賊は「異類」を率いてきたとも記載されており、この「異類」を俘囚とは異なる[[蝦夷]]とする説<ref>関口明 1992</ref>や、[[アイヌ]]ではないかとする説がある。
[[6月21日 (旧暦)|6月21日]]（[[7月10日]]）には[[陸奥国]]に兵士を移送するよう解状が奏せられ、[[7月18日 (旧暦)|7月18日]]（[[8月5日]]）になって[[出羽国]]あての官符が2枚発せられ、国庫の武器・戎具を軍士に与えること、[[租庸調|正税]]穀を兵糧とすること、練兵し賊徒を追討すること、[[秋田城]]司[[源嘉生]]に譴責を加えることが指示された（『[[本朝世紀]]』）。
しかし、史料上からは以後の記録は消え、戦闘の経過は不明である。
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 参考文献 == * [[熊田亮介]]他 『秋田県の歴史』 [[山川出版社]]、2001年、ISBN 4634320509 * [[関口明]] 『蝦夷と古代国家』 [[吉川弘文館]]、1992年、ISBN 4642021663 * [[新野直吉]] 『秋田の歴史』 [[秋田魁新報社]]、1989年、ISBN 4870200694
==関連項目== *[[日本史の出来事一覧]] *[[日本の合戦一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub|てんきょうのらん}}
[[Category:平安時代の事件|てんきょうのらん]] [[Category:平安時代の戦い|てんきょうのらん]] [[Category:秋田県の歴史|てんきょうのらん]]

'''天慶の乱'''（てんぎょうのらん）は[[平安時代]]に起きた[[俘囚]]（[[蝦夷]]）の反乱である。
== 経緯 == [[天慶]]2年[[4月17日 (旧暦)|4月17日]]（[[939年]][[5月8日]]）に俘囚が反乱し、秋田城の軍と交戦したことが[[平安京|都]]に馳駅言上されたと『[[日本紀略]]』等に見える。翌日には官符が3枚発せられ、精兵の訓練と要害堅固、国内の浪人を身分の高下にかかわらず動員すること、[[大物忌神社|大物忌神]]の[[鳥海山|山]]が燃えるとの占いがあるので祀り鎮めることが指示された。『[[貞信公記]]抄』によると、反乱した俘囚は[[秋田郡]]に到り官稲を奪ったとある。同書ではこのとき賊は「異類」を率いてきたとも記載されており、この「異類」を俘囚とは異なる[[蝦夷]]とする説<ref>関口明 1992</ref>、[[渡島]]の[[狄]]とする説<ref>熊田亮介 2001</ref>や、[[アイヌ]]ではないかとする説<ref>高橋崇 1986</ref>がある。
[[6月21日 (旧暦)|6月21日]]（[[7月10日]]）には[[陸奥国]]に兵士を移送するよう解状が奏せられ、[[7月18日 (旧暦)|7月18日]]（[[8月5日]]）になって[[出羽国]]あての官符が2枚発せられ、国庫の武器・戎具を軍士に与えること、[[租庸調|正税]]穀を兵糧とすること、練兵し賊徒を追討すること、[[秋田城介]][[源嘉生]]に譴責を加えることが指示された（『[[本朝世紀]]』）。
しかし、史料上からは以後の記録は消え、戦闘の経過は不明である。
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 参考文献 == * [[熊田亮介]] 「古代国家と秋田」 『秋田県の歴史』 [[山川出版社]]、2001年、ISBN 4634320509 * [[関口明]] 『蝦夷と古代国家』 [[吉川弘文館]]、1992年、ISBN 4642021663 * [[高橋崇]] 『蝦夷―古代東北人の歴史』 [[中央公論新社]]、[[中公新書]]、1986年、ISBN 4121008049 * [[新野直吉]] 『秋田の歴史』 [[秋田魁新報社]]、1989年、ISBN 4870200694
==関連項目== *[[日本史の出来事一覧]] *[[日本の合戦一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub|てんきょうのらん}}

[[6月21日 (旧暦)|6月21日]]（[[7月10日]]）には[[陸奥国]]に兵士を移送するよう解状が奏せられ、[[7月18日 (旧暦)|7月18日]]（[[8月5日]]）になって[[出羽国]]あての官符が2枚発せられ、国庫の武器・戎具を軍士に与えること、[[租庸調|正税]]穀を兵糧とすること、練兵し賊徒を追討すること、[[秋田城介]][[源嘉生]]に譴責を加えることが指示された（『[[本朝世紀]]』）。
しかし、史料上からは以後の記録は消え、戦闘の経過は不明である。

'''天慶の乱'''（てんぎょうのらん）は[[平安時代]]に起きた[[俘囚]]（[[蝦夷]]）の反乱である。
== 経緯 == [[天慶]]2年[[4月17日 (旧暦)|4月17日]]（[[939年]][[5月8日]]）に俘囚が反乱し、[[秋田城]]の軍と交戦したことが[[平安京|都]]に馳駅言上されたと『[[日本紀略]]』等に見える。翌日には官符が3枚発せられ、精兵の訓練と要害堅固、国内の浪人を身分の高下にかかわらず動員すること、[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神]]の[[鳥海山|山]]が燃えるとの占いがあるので祀り鎮めることが指示された。『[[貞信公記]]抄』によると、反乱した俘囚は[[秋田郡]]に到り官稲を奪ったとある。同書ではこのとき賊は「異類」を率いてきたとも記載されており、この「異類」を俘囚とは異なる[[蝦夷]]とする説<ref>関口明 1992</ref>、[[渡島]]の[[狄]]とする説<ref>熊田亮介 2001</ref>や、[[アイヌ]]ではないかとする説<ref>高橋崇 1986</ref>がある。

'''天慶の乱'''（てんぎょうのらん）は[[平安時代]]に起きた[[俘囚]]（[[蝦夷]]）の反乱である。
== 経緯 == [[天慶]]2年[[4月17日 (旧暦)|4月17日]]（[[939年]][[5月8日]]）に俘囚が反乱し、[[秋田城]]の軍と交戦したことが[[平安京|都]]に馳駅言上されたと『[[日本紀略]]』等に見える。翌日には官符が3枚発せられ、精兵の訓練と要害堅固、国内の浪人を身分の高下にかかわらず動員すること、[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神]]の[[鳥海山|山]]が燃えるとの占いがあるので祀り鎮めることが指示された。『[[貞信公記]]抄』によると、反乱した俘囚は[[秋田郡]]に到り官稲を奪ったとある。同書ではこのとき賊は「異類」を率いてきたとも記載されており、この「異類」を俘囚とは異なる[[蝦夷]]とする説<ref>関口明 1992</ref>、[[渡島]]の[[狄]]とする説<ref>熊田亮介 2001</ref>や、[[アイヌ]]ではないかとする説<ref>高橋崇 1986</ref>がある。
[[6月21日 (旧暦)|6月21日]]（[[7月10日]]）には[[陸奥国]]に兵士を移送するよう解状が奏せられ、[[7月18日 (旧暦)|7月18日]]（[[8月5日]]）になって[[出羽国]]あての官符が2枚発せられ、国庫の武器・戎具を軍士に与えること、[[租庸調|正税]]穀を兵糧とすること、練兵し賊徒を追討すること、[[秋田城介]][[源嘉生]]に譴責を加えることが指示された（『[[本朝世紀]]』）。
しかし、史料上からは以後の記録は消え、戦闘の経過は不明である。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * [[熊田亮介]] 「古代国家と秋田」 『秋田県の歴史』 [[山川出版社]]、2001年、ISBN 4634320509 * [[関口明]] 『蝦夷と古代国家』 [[吉川弘文館]]、1992年、ISBN 4642021663 * [[高橋崇]] 『蝦夷―古代東北人の歴史』 [[中央公論新社]]、[[中公新書]]、1986年、ISBN 4121008049 * [[新野直吉]] 『秋田の歴史』 [[秋田魁新報社]]、1989年、ISBN 4870200694
==関連項目== *[[日本史の出来事一覧]] *[[日本の合戦一覧]]

'''天慶の乱'''（てんぎょうのらん）は[[平安時代]]に起きた[[俘囚]]（[[蝦夷]]）の反乱である。

[[6月21日 (旧暦)|6月21日]]（[[7月10日]]）には[[陸奥国]]に兵士を移送するよう解状が奏せられ、[[7月18日 (旧暦)|7月18日]]（[[8月5日]]）になって[[出羽国]]あての官符が2枚発せられ、国庫の武器・戎具を軍士に与えること、[[租庸調|正税]]穀を兵糧とすること、練兵し賊徒を追討すること、[[秋田城介]][[源嘉生]]に譴責を加えることが指示された（『[[本朝世紀]]』）。
しかし、史料上からは以後の記録は消え、戦闘の経過は不明である。

'''出羽俘囚の乱'''（でわふしゅうのらん）は[[平安時代]]に起きた[[俘囚]]（[[蝦夷]]）の反乱。'''俘囚の乱'''、'''天慶の乱'''とも呼ばれる。
== 経緯 == [[天慶]]2年[[4月17日 (旧暦)|4月17日]]（[[938年]][[5月22日]]）に俘囚が反乱し、[[秋田城]]の軍と交戦したことが[[平安京|都]]に馳駅言上されたと『[[日本紀略]]』等に見える。翌日には官符が3枚発せられ、精兵の訓練と要害堅固、国内の浪人を身分の高下にかかわらず動員すること、[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神]]の[[鳥海山|山]]が燃えるとの占いがあるので祀り鎮めることが指示された。『[[貞信公記]]抄』によると、反乱した俘囚は[[秋田郡]]に到り官稲を奪ったとある。同書ではこのとき賊は「異類」を率いてきたとも記載されており、この「異類」を俘囚とは異なる[[蝦夷]]とする説<ref>関口明 1992</ref>、[[渡島]]の[[狄]]とする説<ref>熊田亮介 2001</ref> や、[[アイヌ]]ではないかとする説<ref>高橋崇 1986</ref> がある。
[[6月21日 (旧暦)|6月21日]]（[[7月24日]]）には[[陸奥国]]に兵士を移送するよう解状が奏せられ、[[7月18日 (旧暦)|7月18日]]（[[8月19日]]）になって[[出羽国]]あての官符が2枚発せられ、国庫の武器・戎具を軍士に与えること、[[租庸調|正税]]穀を兵糧とすること、練兵し賊徒を追討すること、[[秋田城介]][[源嘉生]]に譴責を加えることが指示された（『[[本朝世紀]]』）。
しかし、史料上からは以後の記録は消え、戦闘の経過は不明である。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * [[熊田亮介]] 「古代国家と秋田」 『秋田県の歴史』 [[山川出版社]]、2001年、ISBN 4634320509 * [[関口明]] 『蝦夷と古代国家』 [[吉川弘文館]]、1992年、ISBN 4642021663 * [[高橋崇]] 『蝦夷―古代東北人の歴史』 [[中央公論新社]]、[[中公新書]]、1986年、ISBN 4121008049 * [[新野直吉]] 『秋田の歴史』 [[秋田魁新報|秋田魁新報社]]、1989年、ISBN 4870200694
== 関連項目 == *[[日本史の出来事一覧]] *[[日本の合戦一覧]]

{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{デフォルトソート:てんきようのてわふしゆうのらん}} [[Category:平安時代の事件]] [[Category:平安時代の戦い]] [[Category:出羽国|戦てんきよう]] [[Category:秋田県の歴史]] [[Category:蝦夷]] [[Category:939年の日本]] [[Category:10世紀の戦闘]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''天慶の出羽俘囚の乱'''（てんぎょうのでわふしゅうのらん）は、[[平安時代]]に起きた[[俘囚]]（[[蝦夷]]）の反乱。'''出羽俘囚の乱'''、'''俘囚の乱'''、'''天慶の乱'''とも呼ばれる。
== 経緯 == [[天慶]]2年[[4月17日 (旧暦)|4月17日]]（[[938年]][[5月22日]]）に俘囚が反乱し、[[秋田城]]の軍と交戦したことが[[平安京|都]]に馳駅言上されたと『[[日本紀略]]』等に見える。翌日には官符が3枚発せられ、精兵の訓練と要害堅固、国内の浪人を身分の高下にかかわらず動員すること、[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神]]の[[鳥海山|山]]が燃えるとの占いがあるので祀り鎮めることが指示された。『[[貞信公記]]抄』によると、反乱した俘囚は[[秋田郡]]に到り官稲を奪ったとある。同書ではこのとき賊は「異類」を率いてきたとも記載されており、この「異類」を俘囚とは異なる[[蝦夷]]とする説<ref>関口明 1992</ref>、[[渡島]]の[[狄]]とする説<ref>熊田亮介 2001</ref> や、[[アイヌ]]ではないかとする説<ref>高橋崇 1986</ref> がある。
[[6月21日 (旧暦)|6月21日]]（[[7月24日]]）には[[陸奥国]]に兵士を移送するよう解状が奏せられ、[[7月18日 (旧暦)|7月18日]]（[[8月19日]]）になって[[出羽国]]あての官符が2枚発せられ、国庫の武器・戎具を軍士に与えること、[[租庸調|正税]]穀を兵糧とすること、練兵し賊徒を追討すること、[[秋田城介]][[源嘉生]]に譴責を加えることが指示された（『[[本朝世紀]]』）。
しかし、史料上からは以後の記録は消え、戦闘の経過は不明である。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}

== 関連項目 == *[[日本史の出来事一覧]] *[[日本の合戦一覧]] *[[日本の古代東北経営]]

しかし、史料上からは以後の記録は消え、戦闘の経過は不明である。

== 参考文献 == * [[熊田亮介]] 「古代国家と秋田」 『秋田県の歴史』 [[山川出版社]]、2001年、ISBN 4634320509 * [[関口明]] 『蝦夷と古代国家』 [[吉川弘文館]]、1992年、ISBN 4642021663 * [[高橋崇]] 『蝦夷―古代東北人の歴史』 [[中央公論新社]]、[[中公新書]]、1986年、ISBN 4121008049 * [[新野直吉]] 『秋田の歴史』 [[秋田魁新報|秋田魁新報社]]、1989年、ISBN 4870200694
== 関連項目 == *[[日本史の出来事一覧]] *[[日本の合戦一覧]] *[[日本の古代東北経営]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[天慶の出羽俘囚の乱]] to [[Tengyō Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''天慶の出羽俘囚の乱'''（てんぎょうのでわふしゅうのらん）は、[[平安時代]]に起きた[[俘囚]]（[[蝦夷]]）の反乱。'''出羽俘囚の乱'''、'''俘囚の乱'''、'''天慶の乱'''とも呼ばれる。
== 経緯 == [[天慶]]2年[[4月17日 (旧暦)|4月17日]]（[[938年]][[5月22日]]）に俘囚が反乱し、[[秋田城]]の軍と交戦したことが[[平安京|都]]に馳駅言上されたと『[[日本紀略]]』等に見える。翌日には官符が3枚発せられ、精兵の訓練と要害堅固、国内の浪人を身分の高下にかかわらず動員すること、[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神]]の[[鳥海山|山]]が燃えるとの占いがあるので祀り鎮めることが指示された。『[[貞信公記]]抄』によると、反乱した俘囚は[[秋田郡]]に到り官稲を奪ったとある。同書ではこのとき賊は「異類」を率いてきたとも記載されており、この「異類」を俘囚とは異なる[[蝦夷]]とする説<ref>関口明 1992</ref>、[[渡島]]の[[狄]]とする説<ref>熊田亮介 2001</ref> や、[[アイヌ]]ではないかとする説<ref>高橋崇 1986</ref> がある。
[[6月21日 (旧暦)|6月21日]]（[[7月24日]]）には[[陸奥国]]に兵士を移送するよう解状が奏せられ、[[7月18日 (旧暦)|7月18日]]（[[8月19日]]）になって[[出羽国]]あての官符が2枚発せられ、国庫の武器・戎具を軍士に与えること、[[租庸調|正税]]穀を兵糧とすること、練兵し賊徒を追討すること、[[秋田城介]][[源嘉生]]に譴責を加えることが指示された（『[[本朝世紀]]』）。
しかし、史料上からは以後の記録は消え、戦闘の経過は不明である。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * [[熊田亮介]] 「古代国家と秋田」 『秋田県の歴史』 [[山川出版社]]、2001年、ISBN 4634320509 * [[関口明]] 『蝦夷と古代国家』 [[吉川弘文館]]、1992年、ISBN 4642021663 * [[高橋崇]] 『蝦夷―古代東北人の歴史』 [[中央公論新社]]、[[中公新書]]、1986年、ISBN 4121008049 * [[新野直吉]] 『秋田の歴史』 [[秋田魁新報|秋田魁新報社]]、1989年、ISBN 4870200694
== 関連項目 == *[[日本史の出来事一覧]] *[[日本の合戦一覧]] *[[日本の古代東北経営]]
{{デフォルトソート:てんきようのてわふしゆうのらん}} [[Category:平安時代の事件]] [[Category:平安時代の戦い]] [[Category:出羽国|戦てんきよう]] [[Category:秋田県の歴史]] [[Category:蝦夷]] [[Category:939年の日本]] [[Category:10世紀の戦闘]] [[Category:蝦夷征討]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天慶の出羽俘囚の乱]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11442603]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''The Tengyō Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho''' (天慶の出羽俘囚の乱, Tengyō no Dewa Fushū no Ran) was a rebellion by the {{ill|Fushū|ja|俘囚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushū|qq=|1=fushū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9391282}} (assimilated Emishi) during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It is also referred to as the "Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho," the "Fushū Rebellion," or the "Tengyō Rebellion."
== Events == On {{nihongo|April 17, Tengyō 2|天慶2nd year 4月17日|May 22, 939}}, a rebellion by the fushū occurred, engaging with the garrison at [[:en:Akita Castle|Akita Castle]]. This event was reported to the [[:en:Heian-kyō|Capital of Heian-kyō]] as noted in historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Kiryaku|Nihon Kiryaku]]. On the following day, three imperial decrees were issued, instructing the training of elite soldiers, fortification of strategic locations, mobilization of vagrants regardless of status, and the ritual appeasement of {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, as the oracle predicted that the [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] would burn. According to an excerpt from the {{ill|Teishinkōki|ja|貞信公記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishinkōki|qq=|1=Teishinkōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634497}}, the rebels reached [[:en:Akita District|Akita District]], plundering government rice stores. The same record mentions that the rebels were accompanied by "異類" (irui, "other kinds"), which various interpretations suggest might refer to other Emishi groups<ref>Akira Sekiguchi, 1992</ref>, the {{ill|Watari-shima|ja|渡島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watari-shima|qq=|1=Watari-shima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561909}} (ancestors of the Ainu)<ref>Ryōsuke Kumada, 2001</ref>, or even early [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu people]].<ref>Takashi Takahashi, 1986</ref>
On {{nihongo|June 21|6月21日|July 24, 939}}, it was reported that troops from [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] would be relocated. On {{nihongo|July 18|7月18日|August 19, 939}}, two additional decrees were sent to [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]]. These instructed that weapons and armor stored in the provincial treasury be distributed to soldiers, that grain taxes be used as rations, that troops be trained to suppress the rebellion, and that [[:en:Akita Castle|Akita Castle]] Governor {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinari|ja|源嘉生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshinari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} be reprimanded.<ref>[[:en:Honchō Seiki|Honchō Seiki]]</ref>
However, further historical records of the rebellion cease, and the subsequent course of the conflict remains unclear.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * Ryōsuke Kumada, "The Ancient State and Akita," in ''The History of Akita Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, ISBN 4634320509. * Akira Sekiguchi, ''The Emishi and the Ancient State,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1992, ISBN 4642021663. * Takashi Takahashi, ''The Emishi: A History of Ancient Tōhoku,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsha, Chūkō Shinsho, 1986, ISBN 4121008049. * Naokichi Niino, ''The History of Akita,'' Akita Sakigake Shimbun, 1989, ISBN 4870200694.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of historical events in Japan|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of historical events in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of battles in Japanese history]] * {{ill|Ancient administration of Tōhoku|ja|日本の古代東北経営|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient administration of Tōhoku|qq=|1=administration of ancient Tōhoku by Japanese government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625781}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{Omonoimi Faith}}

On {{nihongo|June 21|6月21日|July 24, 939}}, it was reported that troops from [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] would be relocated. However, further historical records of the rebellion cease, and the subsequent course of the conflict remains unclear.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * Ryōsuke Kumada, "The Ancient State and Akita," in ''The History of Akita Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, ISBN 4634320509. * Akira Sekiguchi, ''The Emishi and the Ancient State,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1992, ISBN 4642021663. * Takashi Takahashi, ''The Emishi: A History of Ancient Tōhoku,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsha, Chūkō Shinsho, 1986, ISBN 4121008049. * Naokichi Niino, ''The History of Akita,'' Akita Sakigake Shimbun, 1989, ISBN 4870200694.

<!--Tengyō Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho--> [[Category:10th-century battles]] [[Category:Japan in 939]] [[Category:Incidents of the Heian period]] [[Category:Battles of the Heian period]] [[Category:Ezo]] [[Category:Ezo]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11442603}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天慶の出羽俘囚の乱]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11442603]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''The Tengyō Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho''' (天慶の出羽俘囚の乱, Tengyō no Dewa Fushū no Ran) was a rebellion by the {{ill|Fushū|ja|俘囚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushū|1=fushū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9391282}} (assimilated Emishi) during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It is also referred to as the "Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho," the "Fushū Rebellion," or the "Tengyō Rebellion."
== Events == On {{nihongo|April 17, Tengyō 2|天慶2nd year 4月17日|May 22, 939}}, a rebellion by the fushū occurred, engaging with the garrison at [[:en:Akita Castle|Akita Castle]]. This event was reported to the [[:en:Heian-kyō|Capital of Heian-kyō]] as noted in historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Kiryaku|Nihon Kiryaku]]. On the following day, three imperial decrees were issued, instructing the training of elite soldiers, fortification of strategic locations, mobilization of vagrants regardless of status, and the ritual appeasement of {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, as the oracle predicted that the [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] would burn. According to an excerpt from the {{ill|Teishinkōki|ja|貞信公記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishinkōki|1=Teishinkōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634497}}, the rebels reached [[:en:Akita District|Akita District]], plundering government rice stores. The same record mentions that the rebels were accompanied by "異類" (irui, "other kinds"), which various interpretations suggest might refer to other Emishi groups<ref>Akira Sekiguchi, 1992</ref>, the {{ill|Watari-shima|ja|渡島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watari-shima|1=Watari-shima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561909}} (ancestors of the Ainu)<ref>Ryōsuke Kumada, 2001</ref>, or even early [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu people]].<ref>Takashi Takahashi, 1986</ref>
On {{nihongo|June 21|6月21日|July 24, 939}}, it was reported that troops from [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] would be relocated. On {{nihongo|July 18|7月18日|August 19, 939}}, two additional decrees were sent to [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]]. These instructed that weapons and armor stored in the provincial treasury be distributed to soldiers, that grain taxes be used as rations, that troops be trained to suppress the rebellion, and that [[:en:Akita Castle|Akita Castle]] Governor {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinari|ja|源嘉生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshinari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} be reprimanded.<ref>[[:en:Honchō Seiki|Honchō Seiki]]</ref>
However, further historical records of the rebellion cease, and the subsequent course of the conflict remains unclear.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * Ryōsuke Kumada, "The Ancient State and Akita," in ''The History of Akita Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, ISBN 4634320509. * Akira Sekiguchi, ''The Emishi and the Ancient State,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1992, ISBN 4642021663. * Takashi Takahashi, ''The Emishi: A History of Ancient Tōhoku,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsha, Chūkō Shinsho, 1986, ISBN 4121008049. * Naokichi Niino, ''The History of Akita,'' Akita Sakigake Shimbun, 1989, ISBN 4870200694.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of historical events in Japan|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of historical events in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of battles in Japanese history]] * {{ill|Ancient administration of Tōhoku|ja|日本の古代東北経営|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient administration of Tōhoku|1=administration of ancient Tōhoku by Japanese government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625781}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天慶の出羽俘囚の乱]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11442603]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''The Tengyō Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho''' (天慶の出羽俘囚の乱, Tengyō no Dewa Fushū no Ran) was a rebellion by the {{ill|Fushū|ja|俘囚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushū|1=fushū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9391282}} (assimilated Emishi) during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It is also referred to as the "Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho," the "Fushū Rebellion," or the "Tengyō Rebellion."
== Events == On {{nihongo|April 17, Tengyō 2|天慶2nd year 4月17日|May 22, 939}}, a rebellion by the fushū occurred, engaging with the garrison at [[:en:Akita Castle|Akita Castle]]. This event was reported to the [[:en:Heian-kyō|Capital of Heian-kyō]] as noted in historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Kiryaku|Nihon Kiryaku]]. On the following day, three imperial decrees were issued, instructing the training of elite soldiers, fortification of strategic locations, mobilization of vagrants regardless of status, and the ritual appeasement of {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, as the oracle predicted that the [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] would burn. According to an excerpt from the {{ill|Teishinkōki|ja|貞信公記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishinkōki|1=Teishinkōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634497}}, the rebels reached [[:en:Akita District|Akita District]], plundering government rice stores. The same record mentions that the rebels were accompanied by "異類" (irui, "other kinds"), which various interpretations suggest might refer to other Emishi groups<ref>Akira Sekiguchi, 1992</ref>, the {{ill|Watari-shima|ja|渡島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watari-shima|1=Watari-shima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561909}} (ancestors of the Ainu)<ref>Ryōsuke Kumada, 2001</ref>, or even early [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu people]].<ref>Takashi Takahashi, 1986</ref>
On {{nihongo|June 21|6月21日|July 24, 939}}, it was reported that troops from [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] would be relocated. On {{nihongo|July 18|7月18日|August 19, 939}}, two additional decrees were sent to [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]]. These instructed that weapons and armor stored in the provincial treasury be distributed to soldiers, that grain taxes be used as rations, that troops be trained to suppress the rebellion, and that [[:en:Akita Castle|Akita Castle]] Governor {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinari|ja|源嘉生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshinari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} be reprimanded.<ref>[[:en:Honchō Seiki|Honchō Seiki]]</ref>
However, further historical records of the rebellion cease, and the subsequent course of the conflict remains unclear.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * Ryōsuke Kumada, "The Ancient State and Akita," in ''The History of Akita Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, ISBN 4634320509. * Akira Sekiguchi, ''The Emishi and the Ancient State,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1992, ISBN 4642021663. * Takashi Takahashi, ''The Emishi: A History of Ancient Tōhoku,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsha, Chūkō Shinsho, 1986, ISBN 4121008049. * Naokichi Niino, ''The History of Akita,'' Akita Sakigake Shimbun, 1989, ISBN 4870200694.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of historical events in Japan|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of historical events in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of battles in Japanese history]] * {{ill|Ancient administration of Tōhoku|ja|日本の古代東北経営|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient administration of Tōhoku|1=administration of ancient Tōhoku by Japanese government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625781}}

On {{nihongo|June 21|6月21日|July 24, 939}}, it was reported that troops from [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] would be relocated. On {{nihongo|July 18|7月18日|August 19, 939}}, two additional decrees were sent to [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]]. <!--Tengyō Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho--> [[Category:10th-century battles]] [[Category:Japan in 939]] [[Category:Incidents of the Heian period]] [[Category:Battles of the Heian period]] [[Category:Ezo]] [[Category:Ezo]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11442603}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天慶の出羽俘囚の乱]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天慶の出羽俘囚の乱]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11442603]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''The Tengyō Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho''' (天慶の出羽俘囚の乱, Tengyō no Dewa Fushū no Ran) was a rebellion by the {{ill|Fushū|ja|俘囚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushū|1=fushū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9391282}} (assimilated Emishi) during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It is also referred to as the "Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho," the "Fushū Rebellion," or the "Tengyō Rebellion."
== Events == On {{nihongo|April 17, Tengyō 2|天慶2nd year 4月17日|May 22, 939}}, a rebellion by the fushū occurred, engaging with the garrison at [[:en:Akita Castle|Akita Castle]]. This event was reported to the [[:en:Heian-kyō|Capital of Heian-kyō]] as noted in historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Kiryaku|Nihon Kiryaku]]. On the following day, three imperial decrees were issued, instructing the training of elite soldiers, fortification of strategic locations, mobilization of vagrants regardless of status, and the ritual appeasement of {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, as the oracle predicted that the [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] would burn. According to an excerpt from the {{ill|Teishinkōki|ja|貞信公記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishinkōki|1=Teishinkōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634497}}, the rebels reached [[:en:Akita District|Akita District]], plundering government rice stores. The same record mentions that the rebels were accompanied by "異類" (irui, "other kinds"), which various interpretations suggest might refer to other Emishi groups<ref>Akira Sekiguchi, 1992</ref>, the {{ill|Watari-shima|ja|渡島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watari-shima|1=Watari-shima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561909}} (ancestors of the Ainu)<ref>Ryōsuke Kumada, 2001</ref>, or even early [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu people]].<ref>Takashi Takahashi, 1986</ref>
On {{nihongo|June 21|6月21日|July 24, 939}}, it was reported that troops from [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] would be relocated. On {{nihongo|July 18|7月18日|August 19, 939}}, two additional decrees were sent to [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]]. These instructed that weapons and armor stored in the provincial treasury be distributed to soldiers, that grain taxes be used as rations, that troops be trained to suppress the rebellion, and that [[:en:Akita Castle|Akita Castle]] Governor {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinari|ja|源嘉生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshinari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} be reprimanded.<ref>[[:en:Honchō Seiki|Honchō Seiki]]</ref>
However, further historical records of the rebellion cease, and the subsequent course of the conflict remains unclear.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * Ryōsuke Kumada, "The Ancient State and Akita," in ''The History of Akita Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, ISBN 4634320509. * Akira Sekiguchi, ''The Emishi and the Ancient State,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1992, ISBN 4642021663. * Takashi Takahashi, ''The Emishi: A History of Ancient Tōhoku,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsha, Chūkō Shinsho, 1986, ISBN 4121008049. * Naokichi Niino, ''The History of Akita,'' Akita Sakigake Shimbun, 1989, ISBN 4870200694.

On {{nihongo|June 21|6月21日|July 24, 939}}, it was reported that troops from [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] would be relocated. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天慶の出羽俘囚の乱]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天慶の出羽俘囚の乱]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天慶の出羽俘囚の乱]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11442603]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''The Tengyō Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho''' (天慶の出羽俘囚の乱, Tengyō no Dewa Fushū no Ran) was a rebellion by the {{ill|Fushū|ja|俘囚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushū|1=fushū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9391282}} (assimilated Emishi) during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It is also referred to as the "Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho," the "Fushū Rebellion," or the "Tengyō Rebellion."
== Events == On {{nihongo|April 17, Tengyō 2|天慶2nd year 4月17日|May 22, 939}}, a rebellion by the fushū occurred, engaging with the garrison at [[:en:Akita Castle|Akita Castle]]. This event was reported to the [[:en:Heian-kyō|Capital of Heian-kyō]] as noted in historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Kiryaku|Nihon Kiryaku]]. On the following day, three imperial decrees were issued, instructing the training of elite soldiers, fortification of strategic locations, mobilization of vagrants regardless of status, and the ritual appeasement of {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, as the oracle predicted that the [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] would burn. According to an excerpt from the {{ill|Teishinkōki|ja|貞信公記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishinkōki|1=Teishinkōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634497}}, the rebels reached [[:en:Akita District|Akita District]], plundering government rice stores. The same record mentions that the rebels were accompanied by "異類" (irui, "other kinds"), which various interpretations suggest might refer to other Emishi groups<ref>Akira Sekiguchi, 1992</ref>, the {{ill|Watari-shima|ja|渡島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watari-shima|1=Watari-shima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561909}} (ancestors of the Ainu)<ref>Ryōsuke Kumada, 2001</ref>, or even early [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu people]].<ref>Takashi Takahashi, 1986</ref>
On {{nihongo|June 21|6月21日|July 24, 939}}, it was reported that troops from [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] would be relocated. On {{nihongo|July 18|7月18日|August 19, 939}}, two additional decrees were sent to [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]]. These instructed that weapons and armor stored in the provincial treasury be distributed to soldiers, that grain taxes be used as rations, that troops be trained to suppress the rebellion, and that [[:en:Akita Castle|Akita Castle]] Governor {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinari|ja|源嘉生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshinari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} be reprimanded.<ref>[[:en:Honchō Seiki|Honchō Seiki]]</ref>
However, further historical records of the rebellion cease, and the subsequent course of the conflict remains unclear.

== References == * Ryōsuke Kumada, "The Ancient State and Akita," in ''The History of Akita Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, ISBN 4634320509. * Akira Sekiguchi, ''The Emishi and the Ancient State,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1992, ISBN 4642021663. * Takashi Takahashi, ''The Emishi: A History of Ancient Tōhoku,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsha, Chūkō Shinsho, 1986, ISBN 4121008049. * Naokichi Niino, ''The History of Akita,'' Akita Sakigake Shimbun, 1989, ISBN 4870200694.

These instructed that weapons and armor stored in the provincial treasury be distributed to soldiers, that grain taxes be used as rations, that troops be trained to suppress the rebellion, and that {{ill|Akita Castle|en|Akita Castle|WD=Q11595283|lt=Akita Castle}} Governor {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinari|ja|源嘉生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshinari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} be reprimanded.<ref>{{ill|Honchō Seiki|en|Honchō Seiki|WD=Q871866|lt=Honchō Seiki}}</ref>
However, further historical records of the rebellion cease, and the subsequent course of the conflict remains unclear.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天慶の出羽俘囚の乱]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天慶の出羽俘囚の乱]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天慶の出羽俘囚の乱]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11442603]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''The Tengyō Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho''' (天慶の出羽俘囚の乱, Tengyō no Dewa Fushū no Ran) was a rebellion by the {{ill|Fushū|ja|俘囚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushū|1=fushū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9391282}} (assimilated Emishi) during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. It is also referred to as the "Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho," the "Fushū Rebellion," or the "Tengyō Rebellion."
== Events == On {{nihongo|April 17, Tengyō 2|天慶2nd year 4月17日|May 22, 939}}, a rebellion by the fushū occurred, engaging with the garrison at {{ill|Akita Castle|en|Akita Castle|WD=Q11595283|lt=Akita Castle}}. This event was reported to the {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Capital of Heian-kyō}} as noted in historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku}}. On the following day, three imperial decrees were issued, instructing the training of elite soldiers, fortification of strategic locations, mobilization of vagrants regardless of status, and the ritual appeasement of {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, as the oracle predicted that the {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai}} would burn. According to an excerpt from the {{ill|Teishinkōki|ja|貞信公記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishinkōki|1=Teishinkōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634497}}, the rebels reached {{ill|Akita District|en|Akita District|WD=Q11595761|lt=Akita District}}, plundering government rice stores. The same record mentions that the rebels were accompanied by "異類" (irui, "other kinds"), which various interpretations suggest might refer to other Emishi groups<ref>Akira Sekiguchi, 1992</ref>, the {{ill|Watari-shima|ja|渡島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watari-shima|1=Watari-shima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561909}} (ancestors of the Ainu)<ref>Ryōsuke Kumada, 2001</ref>, or even early {{ill|Ainu people|en|Ainu people|WD=Q101828|lt=Ainu people}}.<ref>Takashi Takahashi, 1986</ref>
On {{nihongo|June 21|6月21日|July 24, 939}}, it was reported that troops from {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province}} would be relocated. On {{nihongo|July 18|7月18日|August 19, 939}}, two additional decrees were sent to {{ill|Dewa Province|en|Dewa Province|WD=Q876032|lt=Dewa Province}}. These instructed that weapons and armor stored in the provincial treasury be distributed to soldiers, that grain taxes be used as rations, that troops be trained to suppress the rebellion, and that {{ill|Akita Castle|en|Akita Castle|WD=Q11595283|lt=Akita Castle}} Governor {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinari|ja|源嘉生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshinari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} be reprimanded.<ref>{{ill|Honchō Seiki|en|Honchō Seiki|WD=Q871866|lt=Honchō Seiki}}</ref>
However, further historical records of the rebellion cease, and the subsequent course of the conflict remains unclear.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * Ryōsuke Kumada, "The Ancient State and Akita," in ''The History of Akita Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, ISBN 4634320509. * Akira Sekiguchi, ''The Emishi and the Ancient State,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1992, ISBN 4642021663. * Takashi Takahashi, ''The Emishi: A History of Ancient Tōhoku,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsha, Chūkō Shinsho, 1986, ISBN 4121008049. * Naokichi Niino, ''The History of Akita,'' Akita Sakigake Shimbun, 1989, ISBN 4870200694.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天慶の出羽俘囚の乱]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天慶の出羽俘囚の乱]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天慶の出羽俘囚の乱]]
'''The Tengyō Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho''' (天慶の出羽俘囚の乱, Tengyō no Dewa Fushū no Ran) was a rebellion by the {{ill|Fushū|ja|俘囚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushū|1=fushū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9391282}} (assimilated Emishi) during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. It is also referred to as the "Rebellion of the Dewa Fusho," the "Fushū Rebellion," or the "Tengyō Rebellion."
== Events == On {{nihongo|April 17, Tengyō 2|天慶2nd year 4月17日|May 22, 939}}, a rebellion by the fushū occurred, engaging with the garrison at {{ill|Akita Castle|en|Akita Castle|WD=Q11595283|lt=Akita Castle}}. This event was reported to the {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Capital of Heian-kyō}} as noted in historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku}}. On the following day, three imperial decrees were issued, instructing the training of elite soldiers, fortification of strategic locations, mobilization of vagrants regardless of status, and the ritual appeasement of {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, as the oracle predicted that the {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai}} would burn. According to an excerpt from the {{ill|Teishinkōki|ja|貞信公記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishinkōki|1=Teishinkōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634497}}, the rebels reached {{ill|Akita District|en|Akita District|WD=Q11595761|lt=Akita District}}, plundering government rice stores. The same record mentions that the rebels were accompanied by "異類" (irui, "other kinds"), which various interpretations suggest might refer to other Emishi groups<ref>Akira Sekiguchi, 1992</ref>, the {{ill|Watari-shima|ja|渡島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watari-shima|1=Watari-shima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561909}} (ancestors of the Ainu)<ref>Ryōsuke Kumada, 2001</ref>, or even early {{ill|Ainu people|en|Ainu people|WD=Q101828|lt=Ainu people}}.<ref>Takashi Takahashi, 1986</ref>
On {{nihongo|June 21|6月21日|July 24, 939}}, it was reported that troops from {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province}} would be relocated. On {{nihongo|July 18|7月18日|August 19, 939}}, two additional decrees were sent to {{ill|Dewa Province|en|Dewa Province|WD=Q876032|lt=Dewa Province}}. These instructed that weapons and armor stored in the provincial treasury be distributed to soldiers, that grain taxes be used as rations, that troops be trained to suppress the rebellion, and that {{ill|Akita Castle|en|Akita Castle|WD=Q11595283|lt=Akita Castle}} Governor {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinari|ja|源嘉生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshinari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} be reprimanded.<ref>{{ill|Honchō Seiki|en|Honchō Seiki|WD=Q871866|lt=Honchō Seiki}}</ref>
However, further historical records of the rebellion cease, and the subsequent course of the conflict remains unclear.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * Ryōsuke Kumada, "The Ancient State and Akita," in ''The History of Akita Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, ISBN 4634320509. * Akira Sekiguchi, ''The Emishi and the Ancient State,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1992, ISBN 4642021663. * Takashi Takahashi, ''The Emishi: A History of Ancient Tōhoku,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsha, Chūkō Shinsho, 1986, ISBN 4121008049. * Naokichi Niino, ''The History of Akita,'' Akita Sakigake Shimbun, 1989, ISBN 4870200694.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of historical events in Japan|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of historical events in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Japanese battles|en|List of Japanese battles|WD=Q839176|lt=List of battles in Japanese history}} * {{ill|Ancient administration of Tōhoku|ja|日本の古代東北経営|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient administration of Tōhoku|1=administration of ancient Tōhoku by Japanese government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625781}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{Omonoimi Faith}}
{{translated page|ja|天慶の出羽俘囚の乱|version=98623507|comment=Imported full ja history}}
